ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-09-2020 Continued To Dec-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rodel Rodis appeared by zoom for and with Marites Cadiz by zoom.
Dinah Ortiz appeared by telephone for Soledad Diego (Creditor).

Ms. Hilton (Probate Examiner) informed the parties that this matter got continued to December 21st because an amended petition was filed, and it requires notice. Mr. Rodis stated that they had mediation on October 26th, that it was successful and that they have a stipulation between the Petitioner and the Creditor. Mr. Rodis also stated that payment is to be made to the Creditor no later than November 30, 2020, explained that the estate and the Creditor were tenants in common and stated that they reached a mutually satisfactory settlement. The Court informed Mr. Rodis that it can't approve a settlement without a petition and spoke of the Executor's authority to determine if making the payment is in the best interest of the estate. Mr. Rodis was informed that pursuant to the local rules, he can bring in an ex parte petition to approve the settlement, provided filing of the ex parte is approved from the bench. Ms. Ortiz stated that she is okay with an ex parte petition and that she will work with Mr. Rodis to work out a date. The First and Final Account for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to December 21, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Aug-15-2018 Continued To Sep-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rodel Rodis for Marites Cadiz (Petitioner).
Errol Zshornack for Soledad Iniego (Creditor).

The Court noted that a petition for approval of a status report for continued administration has not been filed. Mr. Rodis stated that it is complicated because of the creditor's claim. The Court admonished Mr. Rodis stating that his Client is outside of the time requirement to report to the Court on status of this administration that complies with all notice requirements and with Probate Code Sections 12200 and 12201, that he was told at the last hearing to file the report, that the creditor's claim does not stop him from reporting on status of the estate, that the approval/rejection of creditor's claim that he filed does not approve or reject the creditor's claim, and that without the report, his Client may find that she will have no authority. Status on close of estate is continued to September 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Mr. Rodis that the petition/report needs to be filed no later than September 13, 2018 or the Court will contemplate suspension of his Client. The Court also reminded Mr. Rodis of the October 24, 2018 hearing on the objections to the inventory and appraisal, and encouraged the parties to work on getting this resolved.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation with respect to the creditor's claim. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than October 12 2018. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by October 17, 2018. The Parties are to return on October 24, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. If the matter is not settled, the parties are to meet and confer to address what it is going to take to bring this issue to an end, what the length of the trial will be, etc. The Court informed Mr. Rodis that this does not relieve his Client from filing the petition for approval of report on status of administration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-31-2016 Continued To Sep-20-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Cadiz in Propria Persona. Errol Zshornack, counsel for creditor also present.

The Court stated that this is a petition for probate to administer an estate, that it is concerned that Mr. Cadiz does not have representation as there will be complicated issues between the will and the trust. Mr. Cadiz reported that he is an accountant and has prepared tax returns for his brother, that he was a licensed realtor, that his brother prepared the holographic will and that the trust was prepared by an attorney. The Court stated that the trust is not before the Court. Mr. Cadiz stated that there is a pour-over will. The Court stated that it is not really a pour-over will in that it does not say that whatever is in the estate goes into the trust. Mr. Cadiz stated that he needs to dispose of some of the assets that are not in the trust and that the distribution of assets have been changed based on the holographic will. The Court stated that it does not have this before it and that Mr. Cadiz needs a probate attorney. Mr. Cadiz responded that he needs to be appointed and then he will hire a lawyer. The Court stated that it prefers he hire the attorney first. Mr. Cadiz stated that he had an attorney helping him but needs another due to conflict of interest. The Court stated that he needs to have an attorney represent him, that this will give the court confidence that things will be done correctly. Mr. Cadiz stated that there is only one asset to distribute. The Court stated that they need to figure out if it is in the will or the trust and that this is not a straightforward and simple matter. Mr. Cadiz responded that his brother's assets are quite simple. The Court stated that since he plans to hire an attorney anyway it requests that he do so first. Mr. Zshornack stepped forward and stated that he has been in communication with Mr. Cadiz and that it is in the best interest of the estate and the creditor to have him appointed. The Court stated that there is now also a claim against the estate, that there are procedures that need to be followed and told Mr. Cadiz that he will now want independent representation. Mr. Cadiz stated that in regards to creditors there are no other claims. The Court continued the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary to September 20, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and stated that if the attorney files an appearance it may not be necessary to come to court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
